H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H‑OS‑L1 (n = 95, mean = 7.15, std = 1.78, confidence = strong) leads the over‑stimulation set with 66 % positive outcomes. Qualitative data reports noticeable improvement and high music liking (19 / 95). YouTube reach is highest among HEALING (30 views) but retention metrics are absent. AutoGluon predicts 6.86 for overstim, lower than observed, suggesting the protocol outperforms model expectations. Distribution is slightly right‑skewed but not bimodal.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Over‑stimulation listeners report noticeable improvement and really like the music; it’s the most viewed protocol.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H‑OS‑L2 (n = 51, mean = 6.92, std = 1.73, confidence = strong) shows 65 % positive scores, with mixed music opinions and balanced rhythm feedback. Settling times average about 10 min. YouTube views are low (5). AutoGluon’s overstim prediction (6.86) aligns closely with the observed mean. Distribution is fairly symmetric.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Works well for overstimulation, with many finding the rhythm balanced and the music pleasant.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H‑OS‑L3 (n = 47, mean = 7.04, std = 1.68, confidence = meaningful) yields 64 % positive outcomes; listeners often use it for sleep preparation. Music is generally liked, and rhythm is reported as balanced. YouTube reach modest (8 views). AutoGluon’s overstim score (6.86) is slightly below observed, indicating modest over‑performance. Distribution shows a single mode near 7.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Helps people unwind before sleep; most find it noticeable and the music agreeable.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H‑AX‑L1 (n = 67, mean = 7.22, std = 1.93, confidence = strong) shows solid efficacy for anxiety, with 63 % positive ratings. Qualitative notes highlight 10‑minute settling, high music liking (10 + 6) and balanced rhythm, especially among self‑listeners and caregivers. YouTube exposure is minimal (7 views, no retention data), suggesting limited discoverability. AutoGluon predicts a higher anxiety score (7.84), modestly above the observed mean, indicating room for incremental improvement. The distribution is fairly unimodal, without clear bimodality.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: This anxiety protocol helps most listeners feel better within about ten minutes, and many find the music soothing.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H‑AX‑L2 (n = 48, mean = 6.94, std = 1.62, confidence = meaningful) yields slightly lower anxiety relief than L1, with 67 % positive outcomes. Listeners report mixed music opinions (neutral to liked) and variable settle times (5–20 min). YouTube views are scarce (6), limiting reach insight. AutoGluon’s anxiety prediction (7.84) exceeds the observed mean, hinting at contextual damping. Distribution shows a modest left‑skew but no strong bimodality.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: A similar anxiety track works fairly well, though some people are neutral about the music and settle at different times.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H‑AX‑L3 (n = 36, mean = 7.08, std = 1.89, confidence = meaningful) maintains comparable anxiety benefits, with 61 % positive scores. Qualitative feedback emphasizes very settling rhythm (10 mentions) and typical 10‑minute settle time. YouTube reach is very low (4 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.84, again higher than observed, suggesting external factors modestly suppress performance. Distribution is relatively smooth, lacking clear multimodal features.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners generally notice a calming effect after ten minutes, with the rhythm feeling very soothing for many.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H‑CR‑L1 (n = 43, mean = 6.35, std = 1.70, confidence = meaningful) serves acute distress; 53 % report positive change. Listeners often note rapid settling (5 min) and strong music liking. YouTube views are limited (6). AutoGluon’s crisis zone prediction (7.96 via H‑CR‑L2) is substantially above the observed mean, flagging a model‑data gap. Distribution hints at a minor secondary peak around 8, suggesting possible bimodality.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: In crisis moments, this protocol provides modest relief quickly, and most people like the sound.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H‑CR‑L2 (n = 33, mean = 6.52, std = 1.70, confidence = meaningful) improves slightly over L1 (55 % positive). Qualitative comments split between love and neutral music feelings. YouTube exposure is low (4 views). AutoGluon predicts crisis outcomes near 7.96, again higher than observed, reinforcing a predictive mismatch. Distribution appears unimodal, centered near 7.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: A slightly better crisis version, with quick settling and mixed feelings about the music.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H‑CR‑L3 (n = 27, mean = 6.52, std = 1.91, confidence = meaningful) mirrors L2 with 52 % positive scores and rapid settling (often within a minute). Some listeners reported dislike of the music. YouTube views modest (5). AutoGluon’s crisis prediction (7.96) exceeds observed mean, indicating potential under‑performance. The distribution shows hints of bimodality, flagged by multiple analysts.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Another crisis track that settles fast for many, though a few listeners didn’t enjoy the music.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H‑SL‑L1 (n = 54, mean = 7.19, std = 1.86, confidence = strong) provides 65 % positive sleep/rest benefits, with quick 5‑minute settling for many. Music is well‑received (13 liked). YouTube views are limited (8). AutoGluon predicts sleep score 7.54, close to the observed mean. Distribution is right‑skewed but not bimodal.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Sleep‑rest listeners settle quickly and appreciate the calm music, reporting overall improvement.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H‑SL‑L2 (n = 45, mean = 7.09, std = 1.55, confidence = meaningful) maintains 67 % positive ratings; however, caregiver/clinician respondents dominate, potentially biasing scores. Music opinions are neutral for many. YouTube views are low (5). AutoGluon’s sleep prediction (7.54) aligns with observed. Distribution is tight around 7.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Sleep protocol shows good results, though many responses come from caregivers reporting on others.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H‑SL‑L3 (n = 28, mean = 7.07, std = 1.68, confidence = meaningful) delivers 64 % positive outcomes, with listeners noting 5‑minute settle times and mixed music opinions. YouTube exposure is minimal (3 views). AutoGluon’s sleep score (7.54) exceeds the observed mean modestly. Distribution is unimodal.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Provides steady sleep benefits with quick settling, though it’s less widely seen.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T‑FC‑L1 (n = 41, mean = 6.46, std = 1.82, confidence = meaningful) is the lowest Focus & Clarity performer (56 % positive). Qualitative notes cite "slightly busy" rhythm as a friction point. YouTube views are low (5). AutoGluon predicts focus score 7.73 for T‑FC‑L3, indicating a sizable gap for L1 that may stem from rhythm density.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus scores are lower here, and some listeners feel the rhythm is a bit busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T‑FC‑L2 (n = 32, mean = 7.19, std = 1.60, confidence = meaningful) rebounds strongly (66 % positive) with balanced rhythm feedback. YouTube views modest (4). AutoGluon focus prediction (7.73) aligns well with observed performance.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Focus improves noticeably, and listeners find the rhythm comfortable.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T‑FC‑L3 (n = 35, mean = 7.63, std = 1.97, confidence = meaningful) attains the highest focus score (71 % positive) and strong music love (8 / 35). YouTube views are the highest in THRIVING (16). AutoGluon predicts focus 7.73, closely matching the observed mean, confirming robust efficacy.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: This focus track is highly effective, with many users loving the music and feeling clear‑headed.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T‑CF‑L1 (n = 34, mean = 6.74, std = 1.73, confidence = meaningful) records 53 % positive creative‑flow scores. Listeners often settle immediately, but music liking varies. YouTube views are very low (2). AutoGluon predicts no specific zone; the observed mean is near the series average, with no clear anomaly.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Creative flow improves a little for half the listeners, with music opinions varying.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T‑CF‑L2 (n = 24, mean = 6.79, std = 1.41, confidence = meaningful) improves slightly (58 % positive). Qualitative feedback includes strong music love from half the sample. YouTube views remain low (2). Performance aligns with series expectations; no strong model‑data gap.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: A modest boost to creative flow; several listeners love the music.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T‑CF‑L3 (n = 18, mean = 7.28, std = 1.32, confidence = developing) shows the highest creative‑flow score (67 % positive) despite limited responses. Listeners report significant improvement and high music liking. YouTube reach modest (7 views). AutoGluon does not provide a zone prediction, but the observed mean exceeds the other Creative Flow items, suggesting potential benefit from longer duration.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Strong creative‑flow gains reported, with many noting significant improvement.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T‑MB‑L1 (n = 25, mean = 7.04, std = 1.34, confidence = meaningful) yields 64 % positive maintenance scores; settle times are long (≈20 min) for many. YouTube views are minimal (3). AutoGluon predicts maintenance 7.69 via T‑MB‑L3, slightly above observed, suggesting L1 may need longer entrainment.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Maintenance listeners report good benefit but need longer time to settle.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T‑MB‑L2 (n = 22, mean = 6.68, std = 2.03, confidence = meaningful) shows broader spread and lower mean (59 % positive). Rhythm opinions are mixed. YouTube views (4) are low. AutoGluon’s maintenance prediction (7.69) exceeds observed, highlighting under‑performance.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Shows mixed maintenance results with a broader spread of experiences.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T‑MB‑L3 (n = 14, mean = 6.93, std = 1.73, confidence = developing) records 71 % positive despite small sample; some listeners report lack of settling. YouTube views (3). AutoGluon predicts 7.69, higher than observed, indicating potential benefit from protocol tweaks.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Despite few users, many report strong maintenance benefits, though a few didn’t feel settled.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X‑ME‑L1 (n = 39, mean = 6.74, std = 1.94, confidence = meaningful) yields 54 % positive mood & energy scores; music is loved by many (10 + 6). YouTube views (8). AutoGluon predicts a low‑zone score of 8.23, markedly higher than observed, indicating potential under‑capture of efficacy in the data.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Mood and energy improve modestly; many listeners love the music, yet scores are lower than expected.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X‑ME‑L2 (n = 25, mean = 6.76, std = 1.71, confidence = meaningful) improves slightly (64 % positive) with strong music love (6 + 6). YouTube views (8). AutoGluon predicts 8.23, again above observed, suggesting room for protocol optimization.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Slightly better mood outcomes, with strong music appreciation.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X‑ME‑L3 (n = 18, mean = 6.28, std = 1.74, confidence = developing) records the lowest mood score (56 % positive) and some no‑effect reports. Rhythm is sometimes described as very settling, but also mixed. YouTube views (4). AutoGluon predicts 8.23, a sizable gap, highlighting a possible ascent‑gradient issue.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: The lowest mood scores in this group; some listeners report no effect.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X‑MD‑L1 (n = 22, mean = 6.36, std = 2.01, confidence = meaningful) shows the lowest motivation score (45 % positive) with a wide spread. Listeners note quick settle times but mixed music liking. YouTube views (5). AutoGluon predicts motivation 7.88, a substantial positive delta, flagging a strong efficacy gap.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation scores are low here, and listeners feel the track isn’t as effective as expected.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X‑MD‑L2 (n = 14, mean = 6.21, std = 1.63, confidence = developing) further underperforms (43 % positive) with modest spread. Settling often exceeds 10 min. YouTube views (7). AutoGluon predicts motivation 7.88, again far above observed, reinforcing a design issue.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Motivation improves a bit less; listeners need longer settling times.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Motivation picks up with this variant, but results vary widely across users.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X‑MC‑L1 (n = 24, mean = 6.75, std = 1.62, confidence = meaningful) achieves 54 % positive mind‑clarity scores; two respondents reported worsening effects. Music is largely loved (9 + 9). YouTube views (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for mind‑clarity via X‑MC‑L2, indicating a modest gap.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X‑MC‑L2 (n = 16, mean = 6.81, std = 1.64, confidence = developing) improves to 56 % positive, with very settling rhythm noted by most. YouTube views are relatively high for TRANSFORMING (12). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, suggesting room for improvement.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X‑MC‑L3 (n = 10, mean = 5.70, std = 2.45, confidence = developing) records the lowest mind‑clarity outcome (50 % positive) and high variability. Qualitative feedback mentions "too busy" rhythm for several listeners. YouTube views (8) are moderate, but retention data lacks. AutoGluon does not assign a specific zone; the large mean‑prediction gap signals a design‑population mismatch.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset shows generally positive outcomes across all three series, with expected patterns of stability in HEALING, high peaks in THRIVING, and broader spread in TRANSFORMING.
• HEALING protocols achieve the highest consistency (mean ≈ 6.98) and low variance, fitting nervous‑system regulation goals.
• THRIVING holds the highest observed means, especially in Focus & Clarity (T‑FC‑L3 = 7.63), aligning with cognitive‑emotional optimisation.
• TRANSFORMING displays the lowest mean (6.59) and highest standard deviations, reflecting its ascending‑arc design for low‑affect states.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series‑level patterns largely follow their therapeutic intents.
• HEALING shows stable scores across diverse entry points, with modest spreads and reliable positive feedback.
• THRIVING exhibits strong peaks in Hold protocols (focus, maintenance) while some Creative Flow items lag slightly.
• TRANSFORMING presents the widest variability; Mind & Clarity and Motivation protocols show divergent performance, suggesting population mismatch or design tension.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols deviate noticeably from series norms, warranting focused checks.
• X‑MC‑L3 (TRANSFORMING) records a low mean (5.7) and high std (2.45), with mixed rhythm opinions – a potential design‑population mismatch.
• H‑CR‑L3 (HEALING) displays a hinted bimodal distribution and moderate drift, suggesting subgroup effects.
• T‑FC‑L1 (THRIVING) underperforms its siblings despite similar design, with qualitative notes of "slightly busy" rhythms.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Several high‑signal opportunities emerge for protocol refinement.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MC‑L3 – investigate listener subgroup splits and consider a gentler ascent gradient.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑FC‑L1 – adjust rhythm density or extend the easing‑in phase to reduce the "slightly busy" perception.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MD‑L1 – redesign the ASCENT arc or add a hybrid DESCENT‑into‑ASCENT segment to close the gap with AutoGluon predictions.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑CR‑L2 – extend the hold phase to improve early‑drop‑off signals (low YouTube view % pending).
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑AX‑L1 – refine Adviser routing to better capture therapy‑session use cases.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift is pronounced for many protocols, especially within TRANSFORMING, indicating evolving data patterns.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: Multiple protocols (e.g., X‑MD‑L3, X‑ME‑L2, X‑MD‑L2) show similarity < 0.6, signaling analytical instability.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient embeddings for meaningful clustering at this time.
• Confidence in embedding‑derived insights is moderate due to limited historical runs and sparse clustering.

by_protocol.X‑MD‑L3: X‑MD‑L3 (n = 13, mean = 7.15, std = 2.38, confidence = developing) rebounds to 62 % positive, though sample is tiny and variance high. Listeners report mixed rhythm opinions. YouTube views (7). AutoGluon prediction (7.88) exceeds observed, but drift is extreme (sim = 0.507), suggesting instability.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: X‑ME‑L1 (n = 39, mean = 6.74, std = 1.94, confidence = meaningful) yields 54 % positive mood & energy scores; music is loved by many (10 + 6). YouTube views (8). AutoGluon predicts a low‑zone score of 8.23, markedly higher than observed, indicating potential under‑capture of efficacy in the data.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: X‑ME‑L2 (n = 25, mean = 6.76, std = 1.71, confidence = meaningful) improves slightly (64 % positive) with strong music love (6 + 6). YouTube views (8). AutoGluon predicts 8.23, again above observed, suggesting room for protocol optimization.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X‑ME‑L3 (n = 18, mean = 6.28, std = 1.74, confidence = developing) records the lowest mood score (56 % positive) and some no‑effect reports. Rhythm is sometimes described as very settling, but also mixed. YouTube views (4). AutoGluon predicts 8.23, a sizable gap, highlighting a possible ascent‑gradient issue.
by_protocol.X‑MC‑L1: X‑MC‑L1 (n = 24, mean = 6.75, std = 1.62, confidence = meaningful) repeats earlier entry; see X‑MC‑L1 above.
by_protocol.X‑MC‑L2: X‑MC‑L2 (n = 16, mean = 6.81, std = 1.64, confidence = developing) repeats earlier entry; see X‑MC‑L2 above.
by_protocol.X‑MC‑L3: X‑MC‑L3 (n = 10, mean = 5.70, std = 2.45, confidence = developing) repeats earlier entry; see X‑MC‑L3 above.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: This anxiety protocol helps most listeners feel better within about ten minutes, and many find the music soothing.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: A similar anxiety track works fairly well, though some people are neutral about the music and settle at different times.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Listeners generally notice a calming effect after ten minutes, with the rhythm feeling very soothing for many.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: In crisis moments, this protocol provides modest relief quickly, and most people like the sound.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: A slightly better crisis version, with quick settling and mixed feelings about the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Another crisis track that settles fast for many, though a few listeners didn’t enjoy the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Over‑stimulation listeners report noticeable improvement and really like the music; it’s the most viewed protocol.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Works well for overstimulation, with many finding the rhythm balanced and the music pleasant.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Helps people unwind before sleep; most find it noticeable and the music agreeable.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Sleep‑rest listeners settle quickly and appreciate the calm music, reporting overall improvement.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Sleep protocol shows good results, though many responses come from caregivers reporting on others.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Provides steady sleep benefits with quick settling, though it’s less widely seen.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Creative flow improves a little for half the listeners, with music opinions varying.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: A modest boost to creative flow; several listeners love the music.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Strong creative‑flow gains reported, with many noting significant improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus scores are lower here, and some listeners feel the rhythm is a bit busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Focus improves noticeably, and listeners find the rhythm comfortable.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: This focus track is highly effective, with many users loving the music and feeling clear‑headed.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Maintenance listeners report good benefit but need longer time to settle.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Shows mixed maintenance results with a broader spread of experiences.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Despite few users, many report strong maintenance benefits, though a few didn’t feel settled.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation scores are low here, and listeners feel the track isn’t as effective as expected.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Motivation improves a bit less; listeners need longer settling times.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Motivation picks up with this variant, but results vary widely across users.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Mood and energy improve modestly; many listeners love the music, yet scores are lower than expected.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Slightly better mood outcomes, with strong music appreciation.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: The lowest mood scores in this group; some listeners report no effect.
